meta.description: Train and evaluate LightGBM gradient boosting models for classification or regression with hyperparameter tuning. Inputs: feature matrix, labels. Outputs: trained model, feature importance ranking, SHAP summary plot, ROC or RMSE performance curves.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated metrics, references, or claims were observed; all reported values came from generated artifacts.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill stays inside model-training and artifact-export scope and does not make direct medical or prescriptive claims.

methodological_ground.detail: Weak-model cases were flagged as caution-only with rerun guidance rather than being presented as valid interpretation-ready findings.

code_usability.detail: The R entrypoint ran successfully across binary, regression, TXT-input, and overwrite-guardrail scenarios.

functional_suitability.note: The core workflow is complete, but the bundled binary presets can finish in diagnostic-only mode more often than the current example framing suggests.

reliability.note: Clear typed validation, specific SKILL_* errors, and strong rerun guidance were observed across success and failure paths.

performance_context.note: Progressive disclosure is good, although the SKILL keeps many large examples inline that could be pushed further into references.

agent_usability.note: The response contract is strong, but a concrete final answer template would reduce agent variance further.

human_usability.note: Trigger language is understandable, but the skill remains fairly CLI-centric and rigid about input shape.

security.note: Input validation and identifier warnings are strong, though exported artifacts and session metadata can still expose raw feature names unless the user reviews inputs carefully.

maintainability.note: The implementation is cleanly split across entrypoint, utilities, model functions, references, and test data.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision and escape hatches are strong; default overwrite behavior slightly weakens idempotency, and example positioning could better separate smoke tests from report-ready runs.

inputs.label: Binary preset on dt_sample1.csv
inputs.note: Run completed and exported all artifacts, but the model collapsed to one class and correctly downgraded itself to caution-only.

assertions.text: Output creates the documented metrics, importance, remediation, figure, and summary artifacts.
assertions.note: All expected files were written under tests/audit_binary_fast.

assertions.text: Output flags weak-model conditions instead of silently presenting a report-ready ranking.
assertions.note: model_quality_flag was caution and interpretation_status was caution_only.

assertions.text: Output includes actionable rerun guidance tied to the detected issue codes.
assertions.note: rerun_hint and remediation rows both contained concrete next steps.

assertions.text: Output stays within the skill's stated LightGBM training and export scope.
assertions.note: No unsupported extra analysis or fabricated interpretation was added.

inputs.label: Regression preset on dt_sample1.csv
inputs.note: Regression workflow executed cleanly and exported an interpretation-eligible result set.

assertions.text: Output resolves the requested regression task correctly.
assertions.note: task_type resolved to regression and regression metrics were exported.

assertions.text: Output produces the documented artifact set.
assertions.note: Metrics, importance, remediation, figure, summary, and session files were written.

assertions.text: Output reports an interpretation-ready status when no quality issues are detected.
assertions.note: model_quality_flag was ok and interpretation_status was eligible.

assertions.text: Output remains reproducible and traceable through saved metadata.
assertions.note: Best iteration, metrics, and artifact paths were preserved in the run summary.

inputs.label: TXT smoke test with Group target
inputs.note: The tab-delimited TXT path worked correctly, and a repeated run produced identical metrics and importance tables.

assertions.text: Output handles tab-delimited TXT input without parser failure.
assertions.note: The loader parsed a 50-row by 201-column table and finished the workflow.

assertions.text: Output generates the requested lightweight binary ranking artifacts.
assertions.note: The run produced the tables, PDF figure, and summary file under tests/audit_smoke_txt.

assertions.text: Output reports an interpretation-ready result when no caution flags are present.
assertions.note: The metrics table reported ok and eligible status fields.

assertions.text: Output is deterministic under the documented fixed seed.
assertions.note: Repeated metrics and importance tables matched with no diff output.

inputs.label: Split-importance binary preset
inputs.note: The split-importance workflow completed, but the bundled example again degraded to a caution-only binary result.

assertions.text: Output generates the requested split-based ranking artifacts.
assertions.note: The split PDF and output tables were written successfully.

assertions.text: Output explicitly downgrades interpretation status when the classifier collapses.
assertions.note: interpretation_status was caution_only and model_quality_flag was caution.

assertions.text: Output supplies concrete rerun guidance for the detected classification failure mode.
assertions.note: The summary and remediation table suggest revisiting class balance and predictors.

assertions.text: Output stays within the promised model-training and export scope.
assertions.note: No extra unsupported interpretation or unsafe behavior occurred.

inputs.label: Overwrite protection guardrail
inputs.note: The first run succeeded, and the second run correctly stopped with SKILL_OUTPUT_EXISTS instead of overwriting a populated directory.

assertions.text: Output stops before overwriting a populated output directory when fail_if_output_exists is enabled.
assertions.note: The second invocation terminated with SKILL_OUTPUT_EXISTS before replacing files.

assertions.text: Output provides a specific and actionable overwrite-protection error.
assertions.note: The message named the target directory and suggested recovery options.

assertions.text: Output still creates the documented artifacts for the initial successful run.
assertions.note: The first invocation wrote metrics, importance, remediation, figure, summary, and session files.

assertions.text: Output behavior matches the documented overwrite contract.
assertions.note: The skill warned or stopped exactly as described in the overwrite section.

key_strengths: The CLI contract is explicit, with strong parameter validation and clear SKILL_* failure messages.
The implementation is reproducible: the workflow seeds its sampling and produced identical outputs on a repeated TXT smoke test.
Weak-model cases are handled responsibly through model_quality_flag, interpretation_status, remediation tables, and rerun hints.
The skill ships a genuinely runnable R workflow with bundled test data, references, and structured output artifacts.

recommendations.title: Reframe weak binary example presets
recommendations.problem: Two bundled binary example paths completed successfully but produced caution-only models. The current example labels make those runs feel closer to report-ready presets than they really are.
recommendations.root_cause: The SKILL documentation does not foreground strongly enough that the small bundled binary datasets are primarily smoke or diagnostic workflows.
recommendations.fix: Rename the bundled binary and split-importance examples as diagnostic or smoke presets, and add a short note that interpretation-ready rankings usually require larger or stronger-signal datasets. Keep the current caution guidance, but move it directly into the example headings and validation section.

recommendations.title: Document session_info artifact
recommendations.problem: The implementation always writes session_info.txt, but the Outputs and Validation sections do not list it. That creates a small documentation-to-runtime mismatch.
recommendations.root_cause: The SKILL.md artifact inventory was not updated when session metadata export was added to the script.
recommendations.fix: Add session_info.txt to the primary artifact list and validation checklist so the documented contract matches the runtime behavior exactly.

recommendations.title: Add final answer template
recommendations.problem: The agent response contract lists required fields, but it does not provide a concrete response shape. Different agents could still vary in how they summarize the same run.
recommendations.root_cause: The SKILL specifies content requirements but not a final reporting template block.
recommendations.fix: Add a short response template showing the expected order for task type, best iteration, primary metrics, top features, quality flags, and artifact paths. Include a mandatory wording pattern for diagnostic-only outcomes.
